chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P.No.D-623 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 12.11.2025 Haji Qalandar Bux Laghari, Advocate for petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh along-with ASI Malik Tasleem Awan, PS Shah Latif Town, Malir Karachi. Mr. Ghulam Abbas, Deputy Prosecutor General, Sindh. == Petitioners have invoked the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, with the narration that they have entered into a free- will marriage with their mutual consent, without any compulsion, coercion or inducement. It is, however, stated that the brother of petitioner No.1, namely Khalid Hussain, arrayed as respondent No.5, being aggrieved of such marriage, has lodged FIR No.1311 of 2025, under Sections 365-B and 34, P.P.C., at Police Station Shah Latif Town, Malir, Karachi. The memo of petition further reveals that petitioner No.1 has categorically stated that she has neither been kidnapped nor is she under any kind of threat but has contracted a free-will marriage with petitioner No.2, and thus seeks protection of her life and liberty as well as her husband petitioner No.2. In response to notice, the SHO, Police Station Shah Latif Town, Malir, Karachi, has submitted a report stating that the aforesaid FIR has been disposed of under “A” Class, while the parties are not turning- up before him so that the statement of the lady be recorded accordingly. It is, however, submitted that as and when the lady appears, her statement will be recorded and the case will then be disposed of in terms of statement of the petitioner lady.

In response to notice, the SHO, Police Station Shah Latif Town, Malir, Karachi, has submitted a report stating that the aforesaid FIR has been disposed of under “A” Class, while the parties are not turning- up before him so that the statement of the lady be recorded accordingly. It is, however, submitted that as and when the lady appears, her statement will be recorded and the case will then be disposed of in terms of statement of the petitioner lady. In view of above, this petition stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish
